title: JLG Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error (9-9 / 99-5) | JLG Official
content_summary: JLG fault codes 9-9 or 99-5 on ES Scissors indicate Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error. The power module (PE) received an out-of-range value in its personality settings. Symptoms: machine fails to start, all functions unavailable. Cause: corrupted personality data in power module EPROM, or failed power module. Solution: (1) Cycle E-stop and key switch to attempt reset. (2) Connect JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) to read full DTC. (3) If personality range error confirmed, reprogram or replace power module via JLG service. Models affected: ES Scissors series (1930ES, 2630ES, 3246ES, etc.).

title: JLG 3246 ES Scissor Lift Flash Code 2-5 Pothole Sensor Fault | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 3246 ES scissor lift fault code 2-5: Drive prevented due to pothole sensor issue. Code breakdown: first number (2) = platform control system, second number (5) = pothole/tilt fault. Cause: pothole bar not fully engaged or misaligned. Solution: (1) Inspect left and right pothole bars — both must be fully engaged and properly aligned. (2) Check pothole bar microswitches for continuity. (3) Inspect wiring harness to pothole switches for damage. (4) If bars are aligned but fault persists, check pothole switch adjustment. Reset: cycle E-stop after correcting pothole bar issue.

title: JLG T350 Flashing Code 9 - Service Required | Heavy Equipment Forums
content_summary: JLG T350 boom lift: Flash code 9 (single flash repeated) indicates machine requires service. Flash code 9 on JLG machines generally signals a system-level fault requiring diagnostic tool access. Procedure: (1) Release all E-stops. (2) Check footswitch engagement. (3) Verify machine is on level ground. (4) Connect JLG Analyzer for complete DTC reading — flash code alone insufficient for full diagnosis. (5) If analyzer unavailable, check battery voltage, inspect control module connections, and review error history if machine has display. JLG T350 uses 4WD articulating boom configuration; check swing/rotation sensor connections.

title: Control System Fault Codes - Genie Z-45/25 Service And Repair Manual Page 102 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie Z-45/25 articulated boom lift control system fault codes from official service and repair manual, page 102. The Z-45/25 uses Genie's standard fault code system with numeric codes covering electrical, hydraulic, sensor and control issues. Fault codes displayed on control module LED or display. General Genie fault code categories: E-codes for OIC (operational indicator), numbered DTC codes for device/circuit faults. Full service manual available on ManualsLib covers Z-45/25 hydraulics, electrical schematics, and complete troubleshooting procedures.

title: OIC and DTC Codes - Genie GS-2646AV Service And Repair Manual Page 92 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-2646AV scissor lift Operation Indicator Codes (OIC) and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) from service manual page 92. OIC codes: LL (off level — do not raise), PHS (pothole protection not deployed). DTC codes from Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) covering 5 categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. GS-2646AV uses same code system as GS-1932/GS-3246 with 272 DTC codes total. Full code chart begins at page 92 of the service manual.

title: Battery Charger Fault Codes and Flash Codes - JLG 1930ES Operator's Manual Page 58 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG 1930ES scissor lift battery charger fault codes and flash codes from operator's manual page 58. Battery charger LED flash patterns indicate: steady green = charging complete; flashing green = charging in progress; flashing red = charger fault. Common charger fault conditions: battery temperature out of range, battery voltage too low/high, charger overheating, bad cell detected. If charger fault: (1) Check battery water level. (2) Check battery connections for corrosion. (3) Verify AC input voltage. (4) Inspect charger connector. JLG 1930ES uses onboard 24V charger system.

title: OIC and DTC Fault Codes - Genie GS-1530/1532 Service And Repair Manual Page 180 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-1530 and GS-1532 scissor lift complete OIC and DTC fault code table from service and repair manual page 180. Two code types: OIC (Operation Indicator Codes) for abnormal operating states, DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Codes) for device/circuit malfunctions detected by SmartLink GSDS. Key OIC codes: LL (off-level), PHS (pothole system). Key DTC categories: hydraulic coil faults, battery/power faults, user interface, sensor/switch faults, and control system. Full code chart with causes and corrective actions on page 180.

title: Genie GS-1930 Fault Codes Complete List and Fix Guide | Reliable Equipment Parts
content_summary: Complete Genie GS-1930/GS-1932 fault code list with descriptions and fixes. OIC codes: LL (off-level — lower platform, move to level surface), PHS (pothole switch fault). DTC codes: E1 (Emergency stop activated — check E-stop buttons), E4 (Low battery voltage — charge batteries, check connections), E6 (Tilt sensor activated — move to level ground, check tilt sensor), E12 (Platform-to-ground control communication loss — inspect CAN bus wiring), E18 (Height limit switch fault — check limit switch and wiring). Coil error codes: 52 (Forward coil), 53 (Reverse coil), 54 (Up coil), 55 (Down coil), 56 (Right coil), 57 (Left coil), 58 (Brake coil) — for coil faults: check coil resistance 20-30Ω, inspect wiring, check valve. Code 18: Pothole guard — check limit switches and harness. Code 68: Low voltage. Reset procedure: lower lift fully, turn off, use override mode, restart.

title: JLG Fault Code 211 - Power Cycle Required on Boom Lifts | GC Iron Support
content_summary: JLG boom lift fault code 211: Power cycle required. Appears on JLG articulating and telescopic boom lifts including 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ series. Code 211 indicates the control system requires a complete power cycle to clear a latched fault. Procedure: (1) Lower all boom functions to stowed/transport position. (2) Press and hold E-stop button. (3) Wait minimum 30 seconds for complete power discharge. (4) Release E-stop and restart with key. (5) If code 211 persists after power cycle, connect JLG Analyzer for underlying DTC. Common underlying causes: control module communication error, sensor data out of range, software fault latch.

title: DTC Introduction and Mast Sensor Default Code - JLG DSP M Operation Manual Page 93 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG DSP M mast-type vertical mast lift diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) from operation and safety manual page 93. Includes DTC system introduction and mast sensor default code documentation. JLG DSP M machines use coded diagnostic system: mast sensor faults display specific codes on the control panel. Mast sensor default code triggers when mast position sensor fails or reads out of range — machine disables lift function. Solutions: check mast sensor wiring, inspect sensor alignment on mast rail, verify sensor connector integrity. Full DTC list with corrective actions on page 93.

title: Comprehensive JLG Fault Codes Guide - JLG 660SJ and 860SJ | ManualsDump
content_summary: Comprehensive fault code guide for JLG telescopic boom lifts, specifically covering 660SJ, 860SJ, and E5002 models. JLG boom lift DTC format: two-part code (X-Y) where X identifies system group and Y identifies specific fault. Group 1 = ECM/engine, Group 2 = platform/basket, Group 3 = sensors, Group 4 = drive system, Group 5 = software/control. Code E5002: engine fault requiring service. Full analyzer-readable fault codes require JLG Analyzer (part 1001249695) connected to diagnostic port. For 860SJ: diagnostic port location is inside ground control box. Key service notes: after fault correction, always cycle power and verify code clears.

title: JLG Fault Codes List Ultimate Troubleshooting Guide | TractorDetailProblems
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG fault codes list covering all JLG MEWP models (scissor lifts, boom lifts, telehandlers). JLG DTC system overview: codes consist of two groups (system group-fault code), accessed via JLG Analyzer tool. Common fault groups: Group 1 (ECM/engine control), Group 2 (platform systems), Group 3 (sensors), Group 4 (drive/traction), Group 5 (control software). General troubleshooting sequence: (1) Note all active DTCs. (2) Start with highest group number first. (3) Check basics — battery, E-stops, footswitch. (4) Inspect wiring for corrosion and damage. (5) Use JLG Analyzer for live data monitoring. (6) Cycle power after each repair to verify code clears.

title: Analyzer Fault Code Listing - JLG 800S and 860SJ Service Manual Page 308 | ManualsDir
content_summary: JLG 800S and 860SJ telescopic boom lift complete analyzer fault code listing from service and maintenance manual, page 308. Contains all analyzer-readable DTC codes organized by group: Group 1 (ECM and engine control module codes), Group 2 (platform/basket controls), Group 3 (sensor faults — angle, pressure, height), Group 4 (drive system and traction), Group 5 (software and CAN bus). Example codes: DTC 001 (all OK), DTC 002 (ground mode OK), DTC 0010 (running at cutback). Requires JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) connected to service port. Full DTC table with descriptions, probable causes, and corrective actions.

title: Default Fault Codes - Manitou Official Diagnostic Portal | Manitou Group
content_summary: Official Manitou Group diagnostic portal default fault codes page. Provides standard/default error codes for Manitou equipment including 180ATJ and other ATJ aerial work platforms, MT telehandlers, and industrial forklifts. This is Manitou's official diagnostic resource — codes listed here are manufacturer-validated. Before using full Manitou MDS diagnostic software, technicians can reference these default codes to identify common fault patterns. Covers engine codes, hydraulic faults, electrical system errors, and control module issues across Manitou product lines.

title: JLG 520AJ Service and Maintenance Manual 3121665 | Issuu
content_summary: JLG 520AJ articulating boom lift complete service and maintenance manual (document number 3121665). This is the full technical service reference for JLG 520AJ technicians. Contains: complete fault code listing with analyzer codes, hydraulic circuit diagrams, electrical schematics, CAN bus diagnostics, component replacement procedures, and calibration procedures. The 520AJ uses JLG's ADE (Advanced Drive Electronics) system with DTC codes readable via JLG Analyzer. Key systems covered: Deutz/Perkins engine fault codes, boom/swing/level hydraulics, platform weighing system, and two-speed drive. Manual number 3121665 is the standard factory service reference.

title: Error Indicator Readout — Fault Code List: Dingli JCPT0307 Operator's Manual Page 29 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Complete Dingli JCPT0307 scissor lift fault code list from official operator's manual page 29. 13 fault codes documented: Code 10 (General Alarm ECU — ECU controller general fault), Code 20 (General Alarm TM1 — Traction Motor 1 controller fault), Code 30 (General Alarm PCU — Platform Control Unit general fault), Code 31 (PCU CPU0 Fault), Code 32 (PCU CPU1 Fault), Code 51 (ECU Alarm), Code 52 (PCU Alarm), Code 53 (TM1 Alarm), Code 54 (Pressure Sensor Error — check lift pressure sensor and wiring), Code 57 (Overload — reduce load below rated capacity), Code 58 (Pothole Protection Activated — move machine to level surface, inspect pothole guards), Code 59 (High Position Limit — boom/platform at maximum height), Code 69 (Power Relay Sleep — system entered sleep mode, restart power, check battery and key switch). Reset procedure: push in and pull out red Emergency Stop button. Same code architecture applies to JCPT0607, JCPT0607DCS, JCPT1208, JCPT1412 series.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 58 (Pothole) | ChinaLiftSupply
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 58 — Pothole Protection Activated. Symptom: machine stops operating or restricts movement on uneven surface. Cause: pothole protection device activated due to uneven/sloped ground surface. Solution: (1) Move machine to level ground. (2) Inspect pothole guards for physical damage or obstruction. (3) Reset guards to normal extended position. (4) Clean debris from guard mechanisms. (5) Check pothole guard limit switches for continuity. After correction, push in and pull out Emergency Stop button to reset fault. Same pothole protection system applies to JCPT1208, JCPT1412, JCPT0607DCS variants. Related codes: Code 57 (Overload), Code 59 (High Position Limit).

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 20 (General Alarm TM1) | ChinaLiftSupply
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 20 — General Alarm TM1 (Traction Motor 1 Controller Fault). Symptom: drive/traction function disabled, machine cannot travel. Cause: Traction Motor 1 controller fault or motor driver issue. Solution: (1) Check battery charge level — insufficient charge can trigger TM1 faults. (2) Inspect TM1 motor controller wiring connections and terminals for corrosion or looseness. (3) Check motor phase wiring for damage. (4) Verify motor controller cooling is adequate. (5) If fault persists, TM1 controller may need replacement. Reset: cycle Emergency Stop button. Related codes: Code 10 (ECU general alarm), Code 53 (TM1 Alarm — more specific TM1 fault).

title: Error Indicator Readout — Dingli JCPT0607DCS Operator's Manual Page 31 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607DCS scissor lift error indicator readout from official operator's manual page 31. DCS variant fault display system. If LED diagnostic readout displays an error code (such as LL), push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button to reset the system. Fault codes follow same numbering as JCPT0307/JCPT0607A series: 10 (ECU), 20 (TM1), 30-32 (PCU faults), 51-54 (alarms and pressure sensor), 57 (Overload), 58 (Pothole), 59 (High Position Limit), 69 (Power Relay Sleep). DCS = DC Slab variant. Full operator manual includes maintenance schedule, hydraulic system, and electrical troubleshooting.

title: Dingli JCPT1412DC / JCPT1612DC Self-Propelled Scissor Lift Operator's Manual | Advanced Access Platforms
content_summary: Official Dingli JCPT1412DC and JCPT1612DC self-propelled scissor lift operator's manual with maintenance information. DC variant (battery-powered) scissor lift. Contains: fault code list (error indicator readout section), maintenance schedule, hydraulic system overview, electrical system checks, safety procedures. Models covered: JCPT1412DC (14m platform height DC variant) and JCPT1612DC (16m DC variant). Fault codes follow standard Dingli code set: 10/20/30/31/32/51/52/53/54/57/58/59/69. Machine specifications: 24V DC motor drive system, hydraulic lift cylinder, 230kg rated load. Available via authorized UK distributor Advanced Access Platforms.

title: Dingli JCPT0607DCM Parameter Manual | Dingli Global (Official)
content_summary: Official Dingli JCPT0607DCM parameter and configuration manual from Dingli Global website. DCM variant of the JCPT0607 scissor lift series. Contains: controller parameter settings, fault code definitions, diagnostic procedures, and technical specifications specific to the DCM variant. DCM = DC Motor variant with specific motor controller configuration. This is a manufacturer-official document from dingliglobal.com — reliability score 1.0. Fault parameters and alarm thresholds configurable via controller interface. Supports same core fault code set as JCPT0607DCS (codes 10-69 range) with DCM-specific motor controller parameters.

title: JLG 450AJ Series 1 — Hydraulic Issues: Loss of Control to Valves | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion on JLG 450AJ Series 1 (Ford engine) hydraulic control loss for multiple valve functions. Problem: Platform Rotate, Platform Leveling Override, Jib, Telescope, Tower Lift, and Steering all lose control simultaneously. Root cause identified: corrosion in electrical connector terminals at harness junction between boom and tractor unit. Over time/outdoor use, connector terminals corrode inside the connector housings, causing intermittent or complete connection failures to the hydraulic control valves. Solution: (1) Inspect harness connectors at front of platform. (2) Check connectors where they pass from tractor unit into boom. (3) Clean corroded terminals with electrical contact cleaner. (4) Apply dielectric grease to prevent future corrosion. (5) Replace damaged connectors. Diagnostic tip: Having a JLG handheld diagnostic tool significantly aids in pulling fault codes from the ECM. Light on platform console flashes MIL codes; light on control board inside ground control box also available.

title: Fault Codes — JLG 450A/450AJ Series II Service and Maintenance Manual Page 343 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG 450A and 450AJ Series II articulating boom lift complete fault code listing from service and maintenance manual page 343 (of 396 total pages). Full DTC table with fault descriptions, probable causes, and corrective actions. Flash code 3/3 (3 flashes, pause, 3 flashes) = basket sensor or joystick calibration fault. Troubleshooting: inspect basket angle sensor for dirt/damage/loose wiring, check joystick calibration, verify all harness connectors are secure. Flash code procedures: (1) Note code pattern. (2) Check MIL light on platform console. (3) Use JLG Analyzer for full DTC reading. Common faults: pothole sensor misalignment, platform angle sensor errors, hydraulic pressure sensor faults, control module communication issues. Reset: cycle E-stop after correcting fault.

title: JLG Fault Codes — Complete Guide and What They Mean | EWP Spares Australia
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG fault code guide from Australian AWP parts specialist EWP Spares. Covers JLG boom lifts including 860SJ telescopic boom. Key content: JLG machines use onboard diagnostic system monitoring inputs/outputs across the control system — when fault detected, code logged and machine restricts/shuts down. Fault codes displayed on platform and/or ground control panel. Diagnostic port on 860SJ located inside ground control box for handset connection. Common fault code examples: ECT (Engine Coolant Temperature) codes — higher than stage 1 limit triggers warning; ECT voltage low/high sensor codes. Three JLG analyzer readers: tethered cable, mobile, and Bluetooth (select models). All models: use analyzer to search fault codes and enable/disable machine options. JLG Knowledge Base (directaccess): 1,500+ articles by fault code or symptom.

title: JLG Boom Lift Troubleshooting Guide | Intella Parts
content_summary: JLG boom lift troubleshooting guide covering diagnosis and repair for JLG articulating and telescopic boom lifts. General troubleshooting approach: document code exactly as displayed on MDI (Multifunction Digital Indicator), perform initial checks (connections/battery/restart), use JLG Knowledge Base directaccess.jlg.com. Step-by-step fault approach: (1) Note all active DTCs. (2) Check battery voltage — low battery causes cascade faults. (3) Inspect E-stops — all must be pulled out and released. (4) Check footswitch engagement. (5) Inspect control module connections for corrosion. (6) Use JLG Analyzer for live data. (7) Cycle power after each repair to verify code clears. Common boom lift faults: sensor out of range (angle/pressure/height sensors), CAN bus communication errors, drive system faults, engine-related codes (on engine-powered machines). Safety: halt operations immediately when fault code appears.

title: Fault Code Chart — Genie GS-2046 / GS-2646 / GS-3246 Service Manual Page 50 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-2046, GS-2646, and GS-3246 scissor lift fault code chart from service manual page 50. Same fault code architecture applies across all three models. Codes documented: 01 (Platform ECM error — replace platform ECM), 02 (Undefined platform DIP switch settings — verify DIP switch configuration), 03 (Chassis up/down switch closed at startup — inspect chassis controls), 12 (Pothole guard failure — check guard deployment and limit switches), 43-57 (Coil errors: 43=forward, 44=reverse, 45=up, 46=down, 47=left, 48=right, 56=brake coil — for coil faults: check coil resistance 20-30Ω, inspect wiring, verify connector integrity), 68 (Low Voltage — charge batteries, check battery connections and condition). Additional code 18: Pothole guard — limit switch fault after replacement, verify wiring harness connections and sensor alignment. DTC system: Genie SmartLink GSDS with 272 total codes. OIC codes: LL (off-level — lower platform), PHS (pothole system status).
equipment_model: Genie GS-2046, Genie GS-2646, Genie GS-3246

title: Genie ECU Fault Codes GEN 5 | Hindley Electronics
content_summary: Official Genie GEN 5 ECU fault code reference document from Hindley Electronics, an authorized Genie service partner. GEN 5 ECU is used in Genie GS-series scissor lifts manufactured during a specific production period. Contains complete fault code table for GEN 5 controller with code numbers, descriptions, and corrective actions. Key resource for differentiating GEN 5 vs GEN 6 fault codes — code numbers may differ between generations. GEN 5 covers models including GS-1930, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-3246 (earlier serial numbers). This PDF is the definitive reference for GEN 5 ECU troubleshooting. Hindley Electronics specializes in Genie/Terex aerial work platform electronic components and repair.

title: Genie ECU Fault Codes GEN 6 | Hindley Electronics
content_summary: Official Genie GEN 6 ECU fault code reference document from Hindley Electronics. GEN 6 ECU represents updated controller generation for Genie GS-series scissor lifts. Updated fault code set with revisions from GEN 5. GEN 6 covers newer serial number ranges of GS-1932, GS-2646, GS-3246, GS-4655. DTC format: numeric codes with 272 total diagnostic trouble codes across 5 categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, controls. OIC codes (operational indicator): LL (off level), PHS (pothole system). Key difference from GEN 5: updated coil fault codes and additional sensor diagnostic codes. This PDF is the definitive reference for GEN 6 ECU troubleshooting. Critical to know which ECU generation your machine uses before referencing fault codes.

title: Manitou Fault Codes List | Scribd
content_summary: Manitou fault codes list document on Scribd covering telehandler and aerial work platform fault codes. Includes fault codes for Manitou equipment lines including ATJ boom lifts and MT telehandler series. Manitou fault code structure: error codes are displayed on the machine's control panel or diagnostic interface. Codes organized by system: engine (Deutz/Perkins), hydraulic, electrical, and control module. For Manitou ATJ aerial work platforms (including 180ATJ): fault codes accessed via Manitou MDS (Manitou Diagnostic Software) or through the onboard display. Common fault categories: transmission faults, hydraulic pressure errors, load sensor faults, boom position sensor errors. Manitou recommends using local sales partners for diagnostic resolution — dealer locator available on manitou.com.
equipment_model: Manitou ATJ series, Manitou MT series

title: Manitou Fault Codes DTC | Truck-Manuals.Net
content_summary: Manitou DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code) index from truck-manuals.net covering Manitou telehandlers and aerial platforms. Fault codes organized by system group for Manitou equipment. Covers Manitou ATJ boom lift series and MT telehandler series. Manitou diagnostic approach: iV (integrated Validation) diagnostic tool or Manitou MDS software required for full DTC reading on newer models. For ATJ aerial platforms including 180ATJ: platform controller (UPC30) handles platform-side faults; boom/drive side codes accessed via main ECU. Engine fault codes follow Deutz SPN/FMI format (SPN = Suspect Parameter Number, FMI = Failure Mode Indicator) on E3 engine models. Hydraulic fault codes cover: pressure sensor errors, solenoid valve faults, cylinder position errors. Electrical codes: battery voltage, CAN bus communication errors.
